trade.summary: Korea-Norway trade is defined by Norwegian energy and maritime exports to Korea, and Korean manufactured goods flowing to Norway. Norway is a significant supplier of LNG to South Korea. Norwegian salmon exports to Korea are growing. Korean shipbuilders (Hyundai Heavy Industries, Samsung Heavy Industries, DSME) build a significant portion of Norway's offshore oil vessels and LNG carriers.
top_products_a_to_b: ships and offshore vessels (built in Korea)

diplomatic.summary: Korea-Norway diplomatic relations are warm and driven largely by economic complementarity — Norway's energy and maritime sector matching Korea's shipbuilding and manufacturing strengths. Norway's Government Pension Fund Global (GPFF) holds Korean equities. Both are liberal democracies aligned in multilateral institutions. Norway participated in the Korean War under UN command.

military.summary: Norway is a NATO member while Korea is a US treaty ally. Both participated in the Korean War (Norway in medical capacity). Korea's defense industry has growing export ambitions in Europe — the K2 tank and K9 howitzer have attracted Norwegian interest as Norway modernizes its land forces. Korea signed a defense MOU with Norway.
military.alliance_status: Aligned democracies — Norway NATO, Korea US ally

financial.summary: Norway's GPFF holds significant Korean equity stakes. Norwegian energy companies have investment relationships with Korean partners. Korea's shipbuilding sector generates substantial revenue from Norwegian offshore oil and LNG contracts. Korean companies (Samsung, Hyundai) have Norway operations.

energy.summary: Norway is a meaningful LNG supplier to South Korea. Equinor supplies Korean utilities. The maritime energy relationship — Korea building Norway's LNG tankers and offshore platforms — creates a unique circular economic relationship. Both countries are interested in hydrogen supply chains.

scientific.summary: Moderate scientific cooperation in ocean technology, shipbuilding engineering, and marine science. Korea and Norway collaborate on offshore engineering research. Growing cooperation on hydrogen and offshore wind technology relevant to both countries' energy transition goals.
